findings.description: P9 retries do not increment trace_graph_call retry_attempt: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:489-638 defaults retry_attempt to 0, while wrappers at src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:722, src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:757, and src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:892 re-invoke through @resilient_http without passing the actual P9 attempt number, so a 503-then-200 retry emits spans with retry_attempt=0 twice, violating the observability spec and D15.
findings.resolution: Propagate the resilience attempt number into GraphClient request execution, for example with a ContextVar set by resilient_http or a retry-attempt callback, and pass it to _send_with_auth_retry/_emit_graph_call_span for get/post/paginate/get_bytes.

findings.description: get_bytes bypasses the 401 invalid_token refresh path: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:940-991 acquires one token, streams once, and raises GraphAPIError for non-success responses without checking WWW-Authenticate or calling acquire_token(force_refresh=True), violating the graph-client Authentication Token Refresh on 401 requirement for binary downloads.
findings.resolution: Route get_bytes through the same authenticated request helper as get/post, or implement an equivalent single force_refresh replay around the streaming request before raising on a second 401.

findings.description: retry_safe=False POSTs can leave the graph breaker stuck half-open: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:780-807 records failures for non-retrying POSTs but never records success, and CircuitBreaker.acquire_admission only clears the probe flag in src/assistant/core/resilience.py:271-279, so a successful half-open send_email/post_chat_message leaves state='half_open' and future calls short-circuit.
findings.resolution: In _post_no_retry, capture the _post_inner result, call await self._breaker.record_success() on success, then return the result; keep the existing failure accounting path unchanged.

findings.description: teams.post_chat_message does not match the spec contract: src/assistant/extensions/teams.py:227-238 exposes a content argument, but the spec scenario calls text='hello'; src/assistant/extensions/teams.py:401-425 also sends {'body': {'content': content, 'contentType': 'text'}} while the spec requires exactly {'body': {'content': 'hello'}}.
findings.resolution: Rename the tool parameter to text or accept text as a backward-compatible alias, and align the POST payload with the specified body shape unless the spec is intentionally revised.

findings.description: MSAgentFrameworkHarness does not consume ContextProvider despite D10/Capability Consumption: src/assistant/harnesses/sdk/ms_agent_fw.py:155-175 calls compose_system_prompt directly and the harness constructor has no context_provider injection path, so custom CapabilityResolver context providers are ignored.
findings.resolution: Resolve the CapabilitySet once for create_agent and use capability_set.context.compose_system_prompt(persona, role) when present, with compose_system_prompt only as the default provider implementation; add a test with an injected custom ContextProvider.

findings.description: Timeout errors are not consistently mapped to GraphAPIError as required: src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:506-525 catches only httpx.ReadTimeout, so ConnectTimeout, WriteTimeout, and PoolTimeout propagate as raw httpx exceptions; get_bytes at src/assistant/core/graph_client.py:957-1037 has no timeout-to-GraphAPIError mapping at all.
findings.resolution: Catch httpx.TimeoutException subclasses in all request paths, map them to GraphAPIError(status_code=None) with distinct error_code/message values, and still emit trace_graph_call before propagating.
